channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: ‘People are fed up with the home invasions:’ Doug Ford says feds taking too long on bail reform bill
Data show racial, language disparities in mandatory Ontario teacher math test
item.description: Results of a math test teacher candidates in Ontario must pass in order to be certified show striking disparities across language, age and racial groups, the province’s teachers’ federation says.

item.title: Premier Doug Ford receives honorary degree from U.S. university
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford received an honorary doctorate from a U.S. university on Saturday.

item.title: Ontario opposition leader calls on Carney to block Ford government’s airport plans
item.description: Ontario NDP Leader Marit Stiles is calling on Prime Minister Mark Carney’s government to block Ontario’s plans around the expansion of Billy Bishop Airport in downtown Toronto, saying she believes the move could result in wider destruction of the Toronto islands.

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Doug Ford says he hasn’t lost his way, despite recent backlash
item.description: Premier Doug Ford said Thursday he does not feel he’s lost touch with the average person, despite a backlash over a scrapped plan to buy a jet and new polling numbers showing his popularity declining.

item.title: Proceeds of crime helping to fund community organizations in Ontario. Here is how
item.description: Cash, cars, and other high-end goods that are seized as part of criminal investigations is money that is being redistributed to 27 organizations as part of a provincial grant program, Ontario’s Attorney General Doug Downey announced Thursday.

item.title: ‘We want to trade with you:’ Doug Ford tells consular officials that Ontario is ready to diversify trading partners
item.description: The Ontario government is looking abroad to expand its trading relationships with other countries as the trade-war with the United States drags on, planning a series of targeted trade missions in the coming months.

item.title: Extended runway land at Billy Bishop could be more than two kilometres long
item.description: The Toronto Port Authority says it is eyeing a runway extension at Billy Bishop Airport in downtown Toronto that could add more than half a kilometre of land in Lake Ontario in order to accommodate jets.

item.title: Advocates urge Ontario to boost special education funding, report kids being excluded
item.description: Advocates are calling for greater staffing and funding for special education in the province in order to address what they say is a lack of adequate in-class supports to allow their kids to fully participate and be safe.

item.title: Doug Ford’s PCs now in statistical tie with Liberals amid jet fallout: poll
item.description: The Ontario government’s decision to purchase and later return a $28.9-million private jet appears to have created turbulence for Premier Doug Ford and his Progressive Conservatives.

item.title: Ontario will not take over entirety of Toronto Islands: transportation minister
item.description: The province says it will not take over the entirety of the Toronto Islands despite the language of its proposed legislation.

item.title: Toronto World Cup seats off FIFA resale site while it retools for price cap law
item.description: FIFA’s marketplace for resale World Cup tickets no longer has seats listed for Toronto matches after a new price cap law came into effect.

item.title: Ontario may ban cellphones outright in schools
item.description: Ontario is considering an outright ban on cellphones in schools, and is looking at restricting social media for kids under a certain age, Ontario Education Minister Paul Calandra says.

item.title: Ontarians with mild hearing loss may soon be able to purchase over-the-counter hearing aids
item.description: Ontarians with mild hearing loss could soon have access to over-the-counter hearing aids, under a regulatory change the government is considering.

item.title: ‘Our members are upset:’ Toronto police union speaks out over Gardiner, DVP changes
item.description: The head of the union representing Toronto police officers says it is both “ineffective and inefficient” to bring in provincial police to patrol the Gardiner Expressway and Don Valley Parkway.
